1 - Microbanking Launch Plan.mp4

All right, so let's dive into this first launch plan that we did for a client called the Microbanking Method. So as you'll see, for all of our launches, we set up the agenda, we go over the executive summary, the product and offers, ideal clients, competitors, funnel review, Facebook projections, creative strategy, campaigns rollout, strategy risks and proposed solutions, and then Q&A from there. So for our executive summary, Rick and their team, Mike G and Mike Williams, they came to us because they wanted to scale, essentially a hedge fund acquisition engine to a million dollars a month. And they had a couple different product price points, $67, $47. They had a bunch of upsells from there, and then they wanted to ascend these people back to $10,000 and $25,000 deals.

Okay, so they had a $97 front end. They also tested $67; $67 ended up being the winner on this, but this was the initial launch plan that I'm going over with you guys. Then order bump for $37, upsell one was $197, worth a three-pay of $77. Upsell two was $99, or a two-pay of $55; upsell three was $97. They had a back end offer of 25K, and then another back end offer of 10K, essentially events, and then they were able to invest into deals with Rick and all that. So let's dive into the ideal clients and buyer personas. So you had Joe, the real estate professional. Joe is 39 years old; he already owns three multi-family properties, long-term holds.

You could see Joe's goals; you could see the hobbies and interests. So Joe really wanted to get out of the slog of fixing and flipping houses every year and generate enough passive income so he can retire off that. The big thing is really understanding your buyer avatars, and when you understand your buyer avatars, it's going to allow you to write much better creative concepts for each and every one of these. All the time I see people who are writing concepts who have zero idea who they're writing to. Sometimes we'll have newer copywriters will submit a test project for us, and I ask them, cool, who is the actual avatar that you're writing to here? And they'll just give me something very vague. Yeah, I'm writing to somebody who's 45 to 60 years old and works in corporate.

And it's like, no, you really, really need to understand who is this exact person is, and ideally you have a face here. Next person is you have the successful Gen X-er, you know, looking to diversify their investment portfolio, generate passive income, looking to invest in additional assets, financial independence, spending time with their friends and their family, all of that. The high-income professional, you know, they have high income, they're ages 35 to 64, both male and female. You know, they are, like some of their fears are hesitant to make any investments for fear of losing what they have, making the wrong investment choice, getting scammed. Then you have the passive income seeker, you know, high-income professional looking to build passive income and all that. So we found we then dive into the competitors.

So you have Pace Morbidge, Emil, Fundrise, Lendit, Integrity Homelanding, whole bunch of different competitors here, and now you have the actual funnel review. So these are all the funnels, and the big thing here that you'll notice is we map this out in a tabular view, so we're able to see each page type, the page link, and the actual event name that is firing here, just so everything is organized. So if we ever have to switch a media buyer, you know, around or whatever, everything is documented, and you know exactly where it is, you know where the lead's firing, where initiate checkout, purchase, page view, all of that. Same thing here and then from there, we are doing projections So these are some of the projections that we put together.

We use a software called Geru, G-E-R-U. com. You guys will see that in the initial training if you got it. And these were just some of the projections that we made. Now, one thing I will say on this, I don't think we were actually doing baselines yet. So this is a little bit older, so I don't believe we had a baseline in here for this specific offer. So we did it based upon, or we weren't documenting the baseline, and we were just projecting forward from there. So you'll see that on the other ones. But essentially, these were some of the CPAs. This is the liquidation rate, book calls, cost per book call, the gross, the net, cash collected, ROAS, and all of that. Then we had our creative strategy.

These were the different launch scripts that we came up with. You have the campaign rollout strategy. So demographics, we had all genders, 45 to 65 plus, United States. You could see the actual UTM parameters because we were using Facebook. Our budget was $1,000 per day. And then we had our initial creative testing campaign So this campaign would be targeting the best performing audiences established so far in the SAT account to pair them up with the creatives we put together to give them the best chance to perform. And then we were doing a lot of lookalikes of all sales in the last 365 days, 1% lookalike of all calls booked. So as an audience testing, we had broad, we had audience four, property investment club, creative real estate investing, audience six, property investment trust.

And then we did real estate investment association, all that. These were the different audiences that we set up for middle of funnel. All website visitors engaged with Facebook and Instapages, video views. And then we mapped out a whole bunch of risks and proposed solutions. So the main risk was compliance out of the gate, making sure that we weren't going to get placed in the special ads category was the number one there. Number two is we had an unseasoned pixel, so we needed to essentially season the pixel and get a lot more purchases for there. The offer awareness, we need to make sure that we were differentiating between us looking for people to fund versus looking for people to partner with us.

We need to educate them a bit on what we were doing, how it's different, and the various ways to make a profit for this method And then we wanted to make sure we were getting good quality buyers, and that came with our MQL process and optimizing on higher quality leads from there. General risk five was they did a, for the backend, they would do a deposit to then the backend sale, so it would take 20 to 30 days for them to make the higher ticket sale on the backend. So we needed to keep a weekly pulse on deposits from there. Okay, so what were the results? We ran aggressive testing, creative testing, and new audiences. We pulled the CPC down, and we lifted the opt-in rates per the plan's average case path to a 550 CPC and lower CPA.

We followed the Facebook rollout with UTMs, $1,000 a day budget scale to scale what works. We dropped the CPA down to about $236. Liquidation rate improved from 34% to almost 54%. Our total contract value revenue climbed from 118,000 to more than $346,000 by month three, and then our ROAS jumped from 2. 9X to 6. 18X on there. So we were able to attract much better clients. We were able to scale much quicker because we were able to cut the acquisition cost in half, and yeah, ultimately it was a pretty big success from this campaign
